codeReview.summary: InMemoryCheckpointStore implementation is correct and complete. All 5 CheckpointStore trait methods are properly implemented: save, load, load_latest, list, and prune. Tests pass (4 in-memory specific tests, 27 total swell-state tests). Build and clippy pass. Implementation uses RwLock-protected HashMaps as specified.

issues.description: clear() method is implemented but not tested. While not critical for a test-only store, coverage could be improved.

No concurrent access tests exist despite the implementation using RwLock for concurrency. The store is designed for concurrent use (dyn CheckpointStore in trait bounds), but tests are all single-threaded.
save() method updates two HashMaps (checkpoints and by_task) non-atomically. A crash between the two writes could leave inconsistent state. Acceptable for in-memory test store but worth noting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature implementation is straightforward and doesn't introduce any new patterns that would need documentation in AGENTS.md. All conventions are followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Implementation in crates/swell-state/src/traits/in_memory.rs follows existing patterns from sqlite.rs and postgres.rs implementations

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the rust-worker skill procedure correctly. Transcript shows proper use of Glob, Grep, and Execute commands. skillFeedback.confirmed followedProcedure: true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows correct skill chain (mission-worker-base → rust-worker), Glob patterns matched expected files, tests were executed and passed

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services were started by the worker. Commands used (cargo build, cargo test) are all documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml documents: build, test, typecheck, lint commands. Worker used cargo build and cargo test which match documented commands.

summary: InMemoryCheckpointStore for tests is correctly implemented. All CheckpointStore trait methods (save, load, load_latest, list, prune) are functional. 4 tests pass covering save/load roundtrip, load_latest ordering, and prune functionality. Build and clippy pass. Minor issues noted: no test for clear() method, no concurrent access tests, non-atomic multi-map updates in save() - all acceptable for an in-memory test store. Feature fulfills VAL-STATE-002.
